summary: Make the bounded cached-paint timeout fixture pass the clean TypeScript prebuild boundary.

decisions.topic: Happy DOM timer callback type boundary
decisions.decision: Narrow the timeout fixture's TimerHandler to the function callback that the production cached-paint script actually supplies when delegating to Happy DOM's bound setTimeout implementation.
decisions.rationale: The exact clean prebuild rejected the test shim because the DOM TimerHandler type also permits strings while Happy DOM's bound method accepts a function. The production oracle supplies a function, so the explicit boundary preserves runtime behavior without weakening the test.

testEvidence.criterion: The timeout diagnostic regression must compile in the same prebuild used by exact clean evidence.
testEvidence.test: Run the repository TypeScript no-emit check and the focused M4 packaged-walkthrough suite once after narrowing the callback at the test-only boundary.

red.outcome: RED: clean prebuild stopped before Sandbox launch with TS2345 at the test-only TimerHandler delegation.

green.command: pnpm.cmd run typecheck && pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/m4-packaged-walkthrough.test.ts --reporter=dot
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript no-emit passed and 21/21 focused tests passed.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged native M4 evidence
nearMisses.confirmation: The release prebuild correctly stopped on TS2345 before any Sandbox session or product walkthrough began.

questions: No owner decision is required; this is a test-only compile-boundary correction.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
